Patch Set 1:

This looks awkward IMHO, I'd prefer implement as described in simplified-interop. We may just re-open all opened O_RDONLY files (or maybe non-create open? this can be a callback registered into import) after recovery is finished and before delayed requests sent, . In this way any time such file is closed, the open request can be released without server interference.

This doesn't look to be a lot of change, but we have to keep  fake transaction for old client.

Patch Set 1:

I totally agree with you that decoupling open replay with ptlrpc request is on the right direction, but I'm afraid that's not a small fix, all the open, close and open lock code need be revised in that manner.

This patch is just to fix the bug in current mechanism, maybe adding ondisk_transno in ptlrpc_body & a connection flag looks overloading, but I can't think of any other better way to resolve the interop problems.

Patch Set 1:

The above is the same as the long term fix described by Andreas: decouple non-create open with ptlrpc replay, instead do it like ldlm lock replay.

Patch Set 1: (4 inline comments)

Some draft comments on this patch.  I haven't finished a full inspection, but thought I should send these instead of letting them sit and grow old.

Patch Set 3: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(12 inline comments)

Patch Set 3: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(14 inline comments)

I think the test is too fragile as it currently is.  Better to rebase this patch on http://review.whamcloud.com/6874 and use the rpc_cache to detect uncommitted open/close RPCs.  That makes the test more robust, and also helps get 6874 landed which is also good.

I would be happy to combine this client-side fix with a  server-side fix (maybe in a dependent patch) to store and return the last_committed value from the highest object version accessed by the client.  That actually increases correctness (because the client has seen newer state, it should know this), and can help fix this problem for older clients as well.

A sanityn.sh test for the server-side fix might look something like:

        # get the starting value for last_committed
        sync; sleep 3; sync
        START_LC=$($LCTL get_param -n mdc.*.import |
                   awk '/peer_committed:/ { print $2 }' | sort -u | head -1)
        # f1 will be 1000 transnos behind f999
        createmany -o $DIR1/$tdir/f 1000

        # get the ending value for last_committed
        sync; sleep 3; sync
        END1_LC=$($LCTL get_param -n mdc.*.import |
                   awk '/peer_committed:/ { print $2 }' | sort -u | tail -1)
        # open and close the f1 file repeatedly on second mount.
        # it should only see the first transno at this point
        for i in $(seq 500); do
              cat $DIR2/$tdir/f1
        done

        # get the last committed for client 2
        END2_LC=$($LCTL get_param -n mdc.*.import |
                   awk '/peer_committed:/ { print $2 }' | sort -u | head -1)

        # check that the client 2 end LC ~= the start LC
        # check that accessing $DIR2/$tdir/f999 increases client 2 LC
Patch Set 3: (1 inline comment)

Patch Set 3: (7 inline comments)

Andreas, I don't not quite see what's the purpose of returning highest accessed object version as last_committed to client,  and this will require client side change, and it'll break old clients as well.

parents.subject: LU-3968 lbuild: Extend script with build for Xeon Phi card

commit.subject: LU-2613 recovery: free open/close request promptly
commit.message:
LU-2613 recovery: free open/close request promptly

- For the non-create open or committed open, the open request
  should be freed along with the close request as soon as the
  close done, despite that the transno of open/close is
  greater than the last committed transno known by client or not.

- Move the committed open request into another dedicated list,
  that will avoid scanning a huge replay list on receiving each
  reply (when there are many open files).

Signed-off-by: Niu Yawei <yawei.niu@intel.com>
Signed-off-by: Hongchao Zhang <hongchao.zhang@intel.com>
Change-Id: I1a25a35fe7a16681368d92d16964680b6209b3ee
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/6665
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Alex Zhuravlev <alexey.zhuravlev@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
